title: Perplexity API Cheat Sheet
description: Essential commands and techniques for using the Perplexity API effectively.

commands.description: Sets the randomness of the output; higher values increase creativity
commands.scenario: When generating creative writing or brainstorming ideas

commands.description: Limits the number of tokens in the response to control output length
commands.scenario: When you need concise answers for a chatbot

commands.description: Specifies the model version to use for the API request
commands.scenario: When you need to ensure compatibility with existing systems

commands.description: Enables streaming of responses for real-time applications
commands.scenario: When building a live chat application that requires immediate responses

commands.command: prompt="Explain the theory of relativity in simple terms."
commands.description: Crafts a specific prompt to guide the AI's response
commands.scenario: When you need a clear and simplified explanation for educational purposes

commands.description: Reduces the likelihood of repeating phrases in the output
commands.scenario: When generating diverse content such as poetry or music lyrics

commands.description: Encourages the model to introduce new topics in the conversation
commands.scenario: When you want to explore a wide range of ideas in a brainstorming session

commands.description: Encourages the model to break down its reasoning process
commands.scenario: When solving complex problems that require detailed explanations

commands.description: Sets the API key for authenticating requests to the Perplexity API
commands.scenario: When making authorized requests to the API

commands.description: Returns the log probabilities of the top 5 tokens for each step
commands.scenario: When analyzing the model's decision-making process for research

commands.description: Defines a stopping sequence to control when the model should stop generating text
commands.scenario: When you need to ensure the output ends at a specific point

commands.description: Sets a timeout for API requests to prevent hanging
commands.scenario: When you need to ensure API requests complete in a timely manner

commands.description: Use specific site operators within the prompt (e.g., 'site:github.com') to force the Perplexity engine to prioritize technical documentation over general web results.
Define a persona in the initial prompt to adjust the tone and depth of the search results (e.g., 'Act as a Senior DevOps Engineer analyzing this log').
Implement regex patterns to extract and map the 'citations' array indices back to the source URLs for building verifiable UI components.
Monitor the ratio of search result snippets to user prompt length to prevent context overflow and ensure the most relevant citations are processed.
Implement exponential backoff specifically for 429 (Rate Limit) and 503 (Service Unavailable) errors to maintain stability during high-concurrency search tasks.
